description: Hard separation rules governing the three truth planes. Enforces immutability of canonical kernels and prevents truth-plane collapse.

canonical_truth.label: Canonical Truth — Immutable Kernel
canonical_truth.description: Scraped and typed manufacturer truth, specification truth, and code-derived assembly definitions. This plane is the source of record. No external process may write, patch, or delete records in this plane.

project_truth.label: Project Truth — Project Variant
project_truth.description: Project-specific mappings, assembly selections, RFI resolutions, approved submittals, and design decisions applied to canonical truth for a specific project. May reference canonical kernels but may not overwrite them.

field_intelligence.label: Field Intelligence — Observed Project Variant
field_intelligence.description: Contractor-originated observations, installer notes, field fixes, inspection findings, and reality capture references. Enriches the project layer with as-built intelligence. May never mutate tier 0 or tier 1 canonical records.

separation_rules.description: No process operating in project_truth may write to or delete a canonical_truth record.
No process operating in field_intelligence may write to or delete a canonical_truth or project_truth record.
Truth-plane collapse — a condition where tier boundaries are merged or bypassed — triggers an immediate system halt.
Field intelligence may only enrich recommendation and revision layers via append operations.
Cross-plane reads are permitted for all tiers. Writes always remain tier-scoped.
